evals.prompt: We're on Node 22 in `my-mastra-app/`. I upgraded `@mastra/core` and now TypeScript says `Property 'memory' does not exist on type 'AgentConfig'`. I don't want guesses — what’s the current API and where do I verify it in our install?
evals.expected_output: Agent directs to embedded docs under `node_modules/@mastra/*/dist/docs/`, SOURCE_MAP or grep; mentions `references/common-errors.md` and version mismatch; does not invent a new AgentConfig shape from memory.
expectations: Response tells the user to verify against installed package docs or types, not training data alone
Mentions embedded docs path pattern (`node_modules/@mastra/.../dist/docs`) or `references/embedded-docs.md`

evals.prompt: I need the official Mastra doc URL for human-in-the-loop workflows and the local skill file that maps 'intent → links.md section' so I can bookmark both.
evals.expected_output: Correct `links.md` anchor or URL from the Workflows / HITL area; points to `references/topic-routing.md` (or `links.md` directly) for intent routing.
expectations: Includes a mastra.ai docs URL for human-in-the-loop or workflows (or defers to `links.md` as canonical index)
Names `references/topic-routing.md` or `links.md` as the local routing artifact

evals.prompt: Add a new agent in our Mastra app using `google/gemini-3-pro-preview`. Boss insists the string is valid today. What do I run before hardcoding the model id?
evals.expected_output: Run `node scripts/provider-registry.mjs` (with provider flag as needed); avoid asserting model availability from memory.
expectations: Instructs to run bundled `scripts/provider-registry.mjs` or equivalent registry lookup

evals.prompt: Codex agent has no `node_modules` in this sandbox — how should I pull current Mastra docs text for context, and what’s the llms-friendly URL pattern?
evals.expected_output: Remote path: `references/remote-docs.md`, `https://mastra.ai/llms.txt`, append `.md` to doc URLs when fetching.
expectations: Mentions appending `.md` to doc URLs for markdown fetch

evals.prompt: Wire `MCPClient` so my Mastra agent can call the Mastra docs MCP server. I’m lost between `@mastra/mcp` and the npx server package — what does the skill say?
evals.expected_output: Points to SKILL.md MCP section + official MCP overview URL; shows or cites MCPClient + `npx -y @mastra/mcp-docs-server@latest` pattern.
expectations: References `@mastra/mcp` / `MCPClient` and the docs server package name
